observations.summary: The description of the smell in Dan's father's room as 'a man rotting from the inside out' parallels other narrative instances where decay and internal corruption are symbolized by foul odors, reinforcing the motif of internal spiritual or psychological decay.

source_evidence.quote: The smell was extraordinary—not just unwashed body, though that was certainly part of it, but something deeper. The smell of a man rotting from the inside out.

target_evidence.quote: the sickly-sweet scent of decay, the smell of forgotten sacrifices and ancient, spilled blood, caught in his throat.

The suffocating emptiness smelled of sulfur and expectant soil, but his father's closed-off room held the precise, dead air of an opened tomb: the stagnant, sharp scent of a destiny that had been rigorously refused.
